overview: A simple single-page web application that serves a static 'Hello World' message to visitors. The architecture consists of a lightweight frontend web application that renders the greeting directly in the browser without requiring backend services. The application is containerized and deployed as a standalone web component.
requirements: The application must display 'Hello World' text to all visitors
The page must load and display the greeting immediately without delay
The application must be responsive and work on both desktop and mobile browsers
The application must serve the same content consistently on page refresh
The application must expose a /health endpoint for monitoring and readiness checks

components.name: hello-world-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Web Application
  version: 1.0.0
  description: A simple web application that displays Hello World
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      description: Returns the health status of the web application
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Application is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
                  timestamp:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
                required:
                  - status
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a static React single-page application in TypeScript that displays 'Hello World' to visitors. Technology stack: Use React 18+ with TypeScript, Vite as the build tool, and a minimal Express server to serve the built static files. Port: The Express server must listen on port 9090. Frontend implementation: Create a React component that renders an h1 element containing the text 'Hello World'. Apply CSS styling to center the text both horizontally and vertically on the page, use a clean sans-serif font, and ensure the layout is responsive for mobile and desktop viewports. The page should have a white background with dark text for readability. Backend implementation: Set up an Express server that serves the React build output from the dist directory at the root path. Implement a /health GET endpoint that returns JSON with status ok and a timestamp in ISO 8601 format. The health endpoint must return HTTP 200. Build configuration: Configure Vite to build the React application for production. Create a Dockerfile with a multi-stage build: stage 1 builds the React app using node:18-alpine and npm run build, stage 2 uses node:18-alpine to run the Express server, copying the built files from stage 1. The container must expose port 9090. File structure: Place all source code in /hello-world-web directory. Include package.json with dependencies for react, react-dom, express, and devDependencies for vite, typescript, @vitejs/plugin-react. Create tsconfig.json with appropriate compiler options. Include a .dockerignore file excluding node_modules and build artifacts. Error handling: The Express server should handle 404 errors gracefully. Ensure the application starts successfully and logs the listening port. Testing: The application must load without errors, display Hello World immediately, maintain state across refreshes, and respond to /health checks within 100ms.
